summary: Paymentum is a Nashville-based merchant services and payment processing company that helps small-to-mid-sized businesses eliminate or sharply reduce the 3-4% they lose to card processing fees, primarily through compliant surcharging and dual-pricing programs plus omni-channel POS hardware, terminals, gateways, and ACH. It serves healthcare practices, restaurants, and retail merchants on month-to-month terms with no long-term contract, and earns on ongoing transaction volume. Deals close through an assisted, rep-led path: a free payments audit or savings-calculator result leads to a booked demo and a side-by-side savings proposal before a merchant agreement is signed.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a newly-signed merchant from agreement to their first live transaction at 0% net cost — the activation moment that confirms the promise and prevents early silent churn.
campaigns.segment: New merchants; branch by vertical (healthcare, restaurant, retail) for setup specifics and POS stack.
campaigns.rationale: Paymentum's sale is assisted and the 'aha' is operational — seeing 0% net cost on a live transaction — so a confident, supported first-week is the single highest-leverage build, and unactivated merchants are the biggest silent-churn risk.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations for go-live, and deliver the one first action that gets them to a live 0%-cost transaction fast.
emails.subject: You're set up — let's run your first 0% transaction
emails.preview: Your terminal, your savings, and the one step to get live today.
keyPoints: Welcome the owner by name and restate the promise: keep the 3-4% you were handing to the card networks
One clear first action — activate the terminal / run a test transaction — framed as a 2-minute step with rep support on standby
Set the 'aha' expectation: see 0% net cost on your next statement, and reply to this email to reach a real person on the onboarding team

campaigns.name: Audit-to-Agreement Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a completed free payments audit or demo into a signed merchant agreement by putting the exact dollar savings in front of the owner.
campaigns.trigger: Free payments audit completed / demo attended; statement analyzed.
campaigns.segment: Audited leads who haven't signed; branch by monthly card volume (higher volume = bigger savings headline).
campaigns.rationale: The audit-to-proposal moment is the core revenue play for an assisted sale — once a merchant sees the concrete annual dollars they'd keep, a no-long-term-contract offer removes the last friction to signing.

emails.purpose: Deliver the personalized savings proposal, quantify the annual dollars kept, and pre-empt the #1 objection (cardholder surcharge pushback) to move them to signing.
emails.subject: Your audit: $[X,XXX]/yr you're handing to the card networks
emails.preview: Side-by-side proposal inside — and yes, it's month-to-month.
keyPoints: Lead with their real number from the audit: exactly what they pay now vs. ~0% net under Paymentum, in annual dollars
Handle the top objection head-on — compliant surcharging is disclosed at point of sale and most cardholders don't blink; cite the network-rule guardrails
Lower the risk to sign: month-to-month, no long-term contract, free terminal for eligible merchants, and a rep on the line to walk through it

campaigns.name: Audit & Savings-Calculator Nurture
campaigns.objective: Keep audit and savings-calculator leads who didn't book warm, and move them toward a booked demo over a multi-week cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Calculate Your Savings completed or free audit requested, but no appointment booked.
campaigns.segment: Leads below the sales-ready threshold; branch by vertical and by the savings figure their calculator/audit surfaced.
campaigns.rationale: Inbound 'free audit' and 'calculate your savings' CTAs are a primary lead source, and without a follow-up track those high-intent leads almost certainly go cold before they book a call.

emails.purpose: Deliver the savings summary they generated and reframe the recurring fee as a fixable, ongoing profit leak — the problem-recognition opener.
emails.subject: Here's the $[X,XXX] a year you're leaving on the table
emails.preview: Your savings summary — and what that number really costs you.
keyPoints: Recap their calculator result with the concrete annual figure, then show what that compounds to over 3-5 years of card fees
Reframe processing fees as the rare cost a business owner has been told is fixed but isn't — surcharging/dual-pricing changes that
Low-friction next step: a 15-minute free audit of their actual statement to turn the estimate into an exact, signable number

campaigns.name: Appointment Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended audits and demos — and make sure the owner shows up prepared so the meeting produces a real savings number.
campaigns.trigger: Appointment booked via web form; upcoming audit/demo; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled audit, demo, or rep appointment.
campaigns.rationale: Appointments are booked through the web form with no visible automated reminders; a simple reminder track recovers attendance immediately (~35% fewer no-shows) and the prep step makes each audit actually convertible.

emails.purpose: Day-before reminder that locks attendance and tells the owner exactly what to bring so the audit yields a precise savings figure.
emails.subject: Tomorrow at [time]: bring your last merchant statement
emails.preview: 5 minutes of prep = your exact card-fee savings number.
keyPoints: Confirm date, time, rep name, and the join link or address; one-tap reschedule to avoid a silent no-show
Tell them precisely what to bring — their most recent merchant processing statement — so the rep can produce a real side-by-side savings number live
Set the payoff: leave the call knowing the exact annual dollars you'd keep, with no obligation and no long-term contract

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate cold audit leads and merchants who went quiet after a demo, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60-90 days of no opens/clicks/replies, or a stalled lead that never re-engaged after a demo.
campaigns.segment: Dormant leads (never signed); separately, demo attendees who went silent — different message for each.
campaigns.rationale: Referral and inbound leads that stall after an audit or demo are a recurring drop-off, and a quick win-back track recovers 5-15% of them while sunsetting dead contacts to keep the sending domain healthy.

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt re-open that reminds the lead of the unclaimed savings and lowers the bar to re-engage with a single question.
emails.subject: Still paying 3-4% on every card swipe?
emails.preview: The savings number we ran for you hasn't gone anywhere.
emails.cta: Pick up where we left off
keyPoints: Remind them of the core value left on the table — the fees they're still paying that Paymentum can wipe out
Acknowledge timing is hard for busy owners and remove friction: no contract, free terminal, one short call to finish the audit
Make the reply easy — a single yes/no question ('want me to re-run your savings?') so a quiet lead can re-engage in one tap
